title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two crew members were rescued after their fishing boat, Legacy, ran aground near Gloucester on Saturday night.
Gloucester Fire Department, Police, Harbormaster, and US Coast Guard coordinated the response at 252 Atlantic Road.
The fishing boat's hull was damaged by 2-4 foot waves, but both crew members were uninjured.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox traded Rafael Devers to the Giants, but the return has not met expectations.
Boston's new acquisitions from the Devers trade have been mostly ineffective or traded away elsewhere.
The Red Sox have started the 2026 season poorly, with a 2-7 record and struggles in both offense and defense.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins have scored three goals in the last three games, impacting their playoff preparations.
19-year-old James Hagens, currently with Providence Bruins, could offer a needed boost to Boston's offense.
Boston's power play struggles with a 15.6% success rate in the last 20 games, ranking 28th in the NHL.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown led the Celtics to a 113-95 win over the Raptors, bolstering their playoff position.
Neemias Queta was instrumental with 18 points and strong defense, boosting his candidacy for Most Improved Player.
The Celtics outperformed the Raptors in rebounds and paint points despite 15 turnovers and a low three-point shooting percentage.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres, 46, sentenced to 36-45 years for repeatedly raping a young relative in New Bedford.
Victim reported hundreds of assaults occurring from age 12, shortly after moving from Ecuador in 2019, said prosecutors.
Turcios Torres charged with multiple offenses, including rape of a child, faces deportation after prison sentence.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her late mother.
Her mother, who passed away from Stage 4 brain cancer, received extended care at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Oakes, now an oncology nurse, is participating in her second marathon to support Massachusetts General Hospital.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man wielding a sword near Northeastern University, injuring several officers and EMS responders.
The incident occurred when police responded to a distress call on Hemenway Street, suspecting a man in mental crisis.
The Suffolk County District Attorney's Office is investigating, following recent violence in the area around Northeastern.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her godmother, Diana, who died of brain cancer.
Dubois qualified for the marathon at the 2025 Lake Erie Marathon and aims to raise funds for MGH's Cancer Care Equity program.
She praises Massachusetts General Hospital for its care and hopes to support families facing similar cancer treatment challenges.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A woman in her 40s died in a Brockton house fire early Saturday on Glendale Ave.
Two individuals were hospitalized following the fire, while investigators consider the cause still undetermined.
Intense flames engulfed the home's second floor, but officials say it initially appears non-suspicious.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed by New Hampshire State Police after shooting at family members and wounding an officer.
Masse shot a Nottingham officer during an attempted arrest in Raymond, leading to a police manhunt and shelter-in-place orders.
The fatal shootout occurred at 10 p.m. when officers found Masse hiding in the woods; an investigation is ongoing.
